top_categories.workout_context: 89ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At White Rose Classic, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
White Rose Classic's hilly Dales roads, pro-peloton pedigree forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 125 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic White Rose Classic's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At mile 60 of 125, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
11,071ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of White Rose Classic. Train it until it feels boring.
11,071ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At White Rose Classic, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Low-cadence force work for 11,071ft of climbing. The slow, grinding ascents where you can't spin—you push.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 125 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 11,071ft of climbing on hilly Dales roads, pro-peloton pedigree does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for White Rose Classic’s 125 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at White Rose Classic. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at White Rose Classic requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on hilly dales roads, pro-peloton pedigree to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, Over Under, and Gravel Specific to prepare you for 125 miles of hilly dales roads, pro-peloton pedigree in Ilkley, West Yorkshire, UK.
